final_value: Prioritise, prototype, deploy and ship internal and client-facing AI agents and AI-assisted workflows.
Integrate agents with HubSpot, Planhat, Slack, the Edflex SaaS platform and internal APIs.
Architect multi-agent pipelines combining curation, recommendation and analytics agents.
Implement guardrails, monitoring, audit logging and human-in-the-loop patterns for critical decisions.
Ensure the consistency and quality of agent outputs in production.
Translate business-team expertise into agent instructions and train colleagues on AI-augmented ways of working.
Measure and communicate business impact, including time saved, leads generated, customer satisfaction improvements and churn reduction.
Define quality, security and compliance standards for AI agents, including GDPR and client-data requirements.
Own the performance-monitoring framework covering accuracy, latency and API costs.
Lead technology monitoring and propose or arbitrate the AI-agent roadmap with the COO and CTO.

stamp_1.check: Each extracted responsibility appears in the explicit day-to-day responsibility list.

stamp_2.check: The list covers prioritisation, deployment, integrations, multi-agent architecture, guardrails, training, impact, compliance, monitoring and roadmap ownership without adding profile requirements.

evidence: Prioritise and deploy AI use cases
Integrate agents across the Edflex ecosystem
Define quality, security and compliance standards

final_value: Hold a Master's degree.
Have more than 3 years of professional experience.
Have 3 to 5 years of professional experience, including 1 to 2 years on LLM or AI automation projects.
Have solid experience with AI orchestration frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI or AutoGen.
Have hands-on knowledge of LLMs such as GPT, Claude or Mistral and of advanced prompt engineering.
Be proficient with REST APIs, webhooks and automation tools such as n8n, Make or Zapier.
Have programming skills in Python and/or TypeScript.
Be familiar with CI/CD and AWS.
Have experience in B2B SaaS environments.
Be able to translate business needs into technical solutions.
Have experience working cross-functionally with non-technical teams.
Possess process-engineering and change-management capabilities.
Communicate clearly and concisely.
Demonstrate autonomy, proactivity and ownership.

stamp_1.check: The summary explicitly requires a Master's degree and more than 3 years of experience, while the profile says 'we expect' the listed skills.

stamp_2.check: The 3-5 year range, 1-2 years of LLM/AI automation experience, frameworks, APIs, programming, SaaS and cross-functional skills are all preserved as required expectations.

evidence: 1 to 2 years on LLM or AI automation projects
